metadata.title: niwo | Cybersecurity
metadata.description: Nikita is a cybersecurity student and freelance ethical hacker specializing in web application security, penetration testing, and digital defense.

Home.heroEyebrow: Offensive security for modern web applications
Home.heroHeadline: When it happens, will you be ready?
Home.heroParagraph: I help startups, developers, and small teams assess modern web applications, validate real weaknesses, and turn findings into clear remediation priorities before they become production problems.
Home.supportLine: Web Application Security · Penetration Testing · Remediation Guidance
Home.servicesTitle: Security support for modern web application risk
Home.servicesDescription: Focused engagements built to uncover meaningful weaknesses, explain risk clearly, and help teams fix the issues that matter first.

approach.title: A calm, practical approach to security work
approach.description: The goal is straightforward: keep scope focused, test realistically, communicate clearly, and leave teams with findings they can actually use.

scope.title: Focused scope
scope.text: Engagements stay centered on the flows, trust boundaries, and actions that carry real application risk.

testing.title: Realistic testing
testing.text: Testing is grounded in how modern web applications are actually attacked, not just checklist coverage.

reporting.title: Clear reporting
reporting.text: Findings are written to be understandable, defensible, and useful to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

remediation.title: Practical remediation
remediation.text: Each engagement is meant to help teams fix issues with confidence, not just collect another report.

webAppSecurity.title: Web App Security
webAppSecurity.text: Focused reviews that uncover the weaknesses most likely to affect real users, access control, and application trust.

penetrationTesting.title: Penetration Testing
penetrationTesting.text: Targeted testing that validates exploitability, clarifies business risk, and prioritizes the findings worth fixing first.

secureDevelopment.title: Secure Development
secureDevelopment.text: Security-minded support for teams that want stronger implementation decisions before avoidable risks reach production.

metadata.title: Web App Security | niwo
metadata.description: Web application security support focused on finding practical vulnerabilities, clarifying risk, and helping teams remediate issues before attackers do.

card.title: Web App Security
card.text: Focused reviews that uncover the weaknesses most likely to affect real users, access control, and application trust.

hero.title: Web App Security that finds the issues worth fixing first
hero.intro: I review modern web applications with a practical attacker mindset, focusing on the places where trust breaks down: authentication, authorization, input handling, exposed functionality, and configuration choices that quietly expand risk.
hero.outcomeText: You get a clearer view of your real attack surface, which weaknesses matter most, and what to change first to reduce risk without slowing everything down.

covers.title: What this covers
items: Application attack surface review across core user flows and exposed functionality
Authentication and session handling weaknesses, including broken assumptions around login, password reset, and session state
Authorization issues such as missing access controls, privilege gaps, and unsafe object access
Input handling problems including injection paths, unsafe processing, and weak validation boundaries
Configuration and deployment weaknesses that increase unnecessary exposure

steps.title: Understand the application and its trust boundaries
steps.text: I start by looking at how the application is meant to work, where sensitive actions happen, and which assumptions the system relies on.

steps.title: Test the areas where weaknesses tend to hide
steps.text: I focus on the routes, states, and edge cases that are most likely to expose security issues in real use, not just obvious checklist items.

steps.title: Turn findings into practical remediation guidance
steps.text: Each issue is written up with context, impact, and clear next steps so the work can move directly into prioritization and fixing.

bestFor.title: Best for
items: Startups preparing to ship or scale a customer-facing application
Small teams that want security review without heavyweight process
Products handling accounts, permissions, sensitive workflows, or business-critical logic
Developers who want findings they can actually work through and fix

deliverables.title: What you get
items: A concise report with validated findings and clear severity context
Practical remediation guidance for each issue
A prioritized view of what to address first
Follow-up clarification on findings and implementation questions

cta.title: Need a clearer view of your application risk?
cta.text: If you want a focused review of your web application before release, after a major change, or simply before attackers find the gaps first, I can help.

metadata.title: Penetration Testing | niwo
metadata.description: Targeted penetration testing for modern web applications with realistic attack paths, validated findings, and actionable remediation priorities.

card.title: Penetration Testing
card.text: Targeted testing that validates exploitability, clarifies business risk, and prioritizes the findings worth fixing first.

hero.title: Penetration Testing built around realistic attack paths
hero.intro: This is focused offensive testing designed to validate how far real weaknesses can go. The goal is not just to list issues, but to understand exploitability, business impact, and what a motivated attacker could actually achieve.
hero.outcomeText: You get validated findings, clearer remediation priorities, and a more realistic picture of how technical weaknesses translate into actual business risk.

covers.title: What this covers
items: Targeted testing of exposed functionality, high-risk workflows, and critical user actions
Validation of exploitable weaknesses rather than surface-level assumptions
Exploration of attack chains where smaller issues combine into larger impact
Risk-based interpretation of findings instead of noise-heavy output
Professional reporting that helps teams make decisions quickly

steps.title: Define the scope around what matters most
steps.text: I align the engagement around the systems, flows, and assumptions that carry the most risk so time is spent where it counts.

steps.title: Test like an attacker, document like a partner
steps.text: During testing, I validate paths that are realistically exploitable and keep enough structure in the process to make the results clear and defensible.

steps.title: Report findings in a way teams can act on
steps.text: The final output is built to support remediation planning, stakeholder communication, and technical follow-up without unnecessary ambiguity.

bestFor.title: Best for
items: Teams preparing for release, launch, or a customer security review
Products that need external validation of exploitable risk
Organizations that want signal over noise from a testing engagement
Applications with sensitive logic, access control complexity, or high-value workflows

deliverables.title: What you get
items: A professional findings report with severity and impact context
Reproduction details and remediation direction for each validated issue
Prioritized remediation guidance based on practical risk
Clear communication suitable for technical and non-technical stakeholders

cta.title: Need targeted offensive testing before it becomes urgent?
cta.text: If you want an external view of how your application holds up under realistic testing, I can help you validate weaknesses and focus remediation where it matters most.

metadata.title: Secure Development | niwo
metadata.description: Secure development support for teams that want to reduce avoidable application risk earlier in delivery and ship with stronger implementation patterns.

card.title: Secure Development
card.text: Security-minded support for teams that want stronger implementation decisions before avoidable risks reach production.

hero.title: Secure Development that helps teams build with fewer avoidable risks
hero.intro: Secure development support is about improving how products are designed, implemented, and shipped so common security problems are handled earlier, with less friction and fewer surprises close to release.
hero.outcomeText: You get more confidence in implementation decisions, fewer avoidable weaknesses carried into production, and stronger alignment between delivery speed and security expectations.

covers.title: What this covers
items: Review of implementation patterns that commonly introduce avoidable application risk
Security-minded guidance during feature development, change planning, and release preparation
Early review of flows involving authentication, authorization, trust boundaries, and sensitive data handling
Practical guardrails that help developers ship with fewer security regressions
Support that keeps security aligned with product delivery instead of disconnected from it

steps.title: Look at the design and implementation path early
steps.text: I review the intended flow, trust assumptions, and likely weak points before they become harder or more expensive to change.

steps.title: Focus on practical changes teams can absorb
steps.text: The goal is not to add process for its own sake, but to improve decisions, defaults, and implementation details in ways that fit the product workflow.

steps.title: Help teams ship with clearer security guardrails
steps.text: The result is a more reliable path from idea to release, with security built into the work rather than bolted on at the end.

bestFor.title: Best for
items: Teams building new products or shipping major feature changes
Developers who want practical security input during implementation
Startups that need stronger security decisions without slowing delivery too much
Products where trust, permissions, and sensitive workflows matter early

deliverables.title: What you get
items: Implementation guidance tied to your actual product and delivery flow
Review notes on risk-prone patterns and areas worth tightening
Clear recommendations developers can apply without heavy translation
A more security-aware delivery path for future work, not just one release

cta.title: Want security support that fits the way your team builds?
cta.text: If you want to reduce avoidable risk earlier and ship with more confidence, I can support the product and engineering work before issues harden into production problems.

metadata.title: About | niwo
metadata.description: About Nikita, the person behind niwo, working across security, AI, systems, and product design.

About.title: The person behind the system
About.intro: Hey, I'm Nikita. I tend to get pulled toward cybersecurity, AI, systems, and product design for the same reason: I like seeing how things actually work once you get past the surface. Most of the time that turns into thinking about trust, failure points, and how to build things that are useful without being careless.
About.signalText: I care about whether something actually holds up - whether it feels trustworthy, makes sense, and works the way it should when it matters.

drives.title: I like understanding the architecture behind the surface.
drives.text: Curiosity is a lot of it, honestly. I like following a system to the point where it starts to show its edges: where it breaks, where it gets fragile, and where the security assumptions stop being theoretical.

building.title: I'm interested in building things that are useful, secure, and clear.
building.text: Lately, I've been focused on the overlap between security, AI, and product design. It's the kind of space that forces better questions - not just whether something works, but whether it holds up, makes sense, and earns trust. I've also been exploring agent-based workflows with tools like OpenClaw and Codex, thinking through how software, automation, and reasoning can come together in more practical, useful ways.

outside.title: I notice the small things that make something easier to trust and use.
outside.text: Things like clarity, pacing, and friction stand out to me, mostly because they affect whether something feels intuitive or frustrating. I like clean interfaces, but only when they actually help something feel easier to use and better thought through.

metadata.title: Imprint | niwo systems
metadata.description: Legal notice for niwo systems. Non-commercial informational website. Professional offers handled privately, outside this platform.

provider.title: 1. Provider Identification
provider.body: Responsible according to Section 5 TMG / EU eCommerce Directive

nature.title: 2. Nature of the Website
nature.body: This website is a non-commercial informational project focused on technology and security research. No goods or services are sold directly through this platform.
nature.disclaimerText: This website does not directly offer paid services. Any professional collaboration or offer resulting from private contact is handled outside this platform, for example by email or a separate contract.

contact.title: 3. Contact
contact.body: For legal notices or formal correspondence, please reach out via legal@niwosystems.com. If a physical mailing address is required for formal service, it will be provided upon request.

liability.title: 4. Liability for Content
liability.body: Content is created with care; however, no warranty is given for completeness or accuracy. Links to external sites are provided for convenience, and responsibility for their content lies with the respective providers.

metadata.title: Vulnerability Disclosure Policy | niwo
metadata.description: How to responsibly report vulnerabilities to the niwo security team.

Vdp.title: Vulnerability Disclosure Policy
Vdp.description: Responsible disclosure guidance for researchers reporting potential security issues.

overview.title: 1. Overview
overview.body: Security is a top priority for us at niwo. We appreciate the work of security researchers and the responsible disclosure of potential vulnerabilities.

scope.title: 2. Scope
scope.body: This policy applies to digital assets owned, operated, or maintained by niwo, including niwosystems.com and associated subdomains.

guidelines.title: 3. Guidelines
items: Do not exploit vulnerabilities beyond what is necessary to demonstrate them.
Do not access or modify user data without consent.
Do not disrupt or degrade our services.
Provide sufficient detail so we can reproduce and validate the issue.

reporting.title: 4. Reporting
reporting.body: If you discover a vulnerability, please report it responsibly by emailing security@niwosystems.com.
reporting.calloutTitle: Include the following information where possible:
items: Description of the vulnerability
Steps to reproduce
Potential impact
Suggested remediation, if available

acknowledgement.title: 5. Acknowledgement
acknowledgement.body: We may publicly acknowledge valid reports that help us improve our security posture. Thank you for helping keep niwo and its users safe.

metadata.title: Privacy Policy | niwo
metadata.description: Privacy policy for this website covering email contact, hosting, and locally hosted fonts.

intro.title: 1. Introduction
intro.body: This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, and protect personal data when you visit this website or contact us by email. We aim to handle information responsibly and transparently in line with applicable data protection law, including the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).

controller.title: 2. Data Controller
controller.body: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy or your personal data, you can contact us using the details above.

dataWeCollect.title: 3. Data We Collect
dataWeCollect.voluntaryIntro: When you contact us by email, we may process:
voluntaryItems: Your name or signature, if you include it
Your company name, phone number, or other contact details, if you include them
The content of your message and any attachments you send
dataWeCollect.voluntaryOutro: This information is used solely to review and respond to your inquiry.
dataWeCollect.automaticBody: When you visit the website, basic technical data may be processed automatically by hosting infrastructure, such as your IP address, request metadata, browser information, and access logs needed for secure delivery of the site.

purposeLegalBasis.title: 4. Purpose & Legal Basis
purposeLegalBasis.body: We process personal data to operate the website securely, respond to inquiries, and protect our systems against abuse or misuse. Depending on context, the legal basis is either our legitimate interests under Article 6(1)(f) GDPR or steps taken at your request prior to entering into a contract under Article 6(1)(b) GDPR.

storageRetention.title: 5. Data Storage & Retention
storageRetention.body: We retain personal data only for as long as necessary to fulfill the purpose for which it was collected, to communicate with you, or to comply with legal obligations. Email correspondence may be retained where needed to document communication or handle follow-up requests.

thirdParties.title: 6. Use of Third-Party Services
thirdParties.hostingBody: This website is hosted using AWS infrastructure. Hosting providers may process technical connection data and server logs as required to deliver the website securely and reliably.
thirdParties.fontsBody: The website uses locally hosted fonts. No font requests are sent to third-party font providers when loading the site.

sharing.title: 7. Data Sharing
sharing.body: We do not sell personal data. Data is only shared where necessary to operate the website, comply with legal obligations, or protect our rights and systems.

rights.title: 8. Your GDPR Rights
rights.intro: Under the GDPR, you may have the right to:
items: Request access to your personal data
Request correction or deletion of your data
Request restriction of processing
Object to processing in certain circumstances
Lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority
rights.outro: To exercise these rights, contact legal@niwosystems.com.

security.title: 9. Data Security
security.body: We take reasonable technical and organizational measures to protect personal data against unauthorized access, loss, misuse, or disclosure. However, no internet transmission or storage system can be guaranteed to be completely secure.

cookies.title: 10. Cookies & Tracking
cookies.body: This website currently does not rely on non-essential tracking cookies or invasive analytics tooling. If this changes in the future, this policy will be updated accordingly.

changes.title: 11. Changes to This Policy
changes.body: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time to reflect legal, technical, or operational changes. The latest version will always be published on this page.

contact.title: 12. Contact
contact.body: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy or about how your personal data is handled, contact legal@niwosystems.com.
